# Conceptual Overview

### Website Overview

#### What is the My Favorite Albums site?

My Favorite Albums is an interactive website that lets you explore and analyze a personal music collection. The site organizes album rankings, ratings, and listening preferences into an easy-to-navigate dashboard with seven different tabs, each offering unique insights into the music data. 

#### Why would I use the My Favorite Albums site?

The website offers several simple ways to analyze and reflect on a personal music collection from a browser, without sorting through spreadsheets of history or coding at all. My Favorite Albums can help you learn about your own or someone else’s musical preferences in a simple, interactive way. 

#### How does My Favorite Albums work?

My Favorite Albums is a web application that was written in R (a coding language) and published to the web using Shiny Apps (a website hosting platform for R applications). The website works by taking input from the user interface and sending it to the backend to complete various data analysis tasks, given the input and some customized album data. 

**![][image1]**

The above image shows how the website works 

#### What can I learn from My Favorite Albums?

With My Favorite Albums, you can:

- View the top-rated albums from any year or year range  
- Compare two artists' album ratings over time with visual charts  
- Identify which artists have the most albums in the collection  
- Find highly-rated albums that aren’t owned on vinyl yet  
- Discover artists' summaries to find artists with high ratings across multiple albums

### Key Terms

- EP: Extended play. An album featuring fewer songs than a typical album, but more than a single song.   
- Vinyl: An analog music storage medium. Often, a flat disc that is inscribed with ridges or grooves that convert vibrations into sound.  
- Live Album: An album with songs that were recorded during a live event, show, or concert.  
- Data: A collection of information often stored as a table.  
- User Interface: The graphical representation of the web app that a user can interact with in their browser.  
- Backend: The technical infrastructure that supports a website and allows it to function.

### System Requirements

To access and interact with the My Favorite Albums website, you will need to use a browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge recommended) and be connected to the internet.   
**Note:** You will not need to download or install anything to access the website.

### Data Overview

The data powering the My Favorite Albums website is customized to whoever published the website. If someone sent you the site, you are likely investigating their musical history. 

#### What data is used?

Each album is only present in the data once, during the year it was released. Each album is associated with a rating (between 1 and 10), and then all the albums for each year are ranked based on their rating. There is also a column to indicate whether the album was recorded live, is an EP, and/or is on vinyl. 

### Scope

You, as the end user, can interact with the published web application through your browser, using dropdown menus, sliders, and buttons to explore the music data without any coding or technical setup. See the image below for an example page of the web application.
